basics.name: Sathya Narayana Bhat
basics.label: DevOps Engineer
basics.summary: DevOps Engineer with 4+ years building and securing cloud-native infrastructure across AWS and GCP. Hands-on with the full DevSecOps stack - Kubernetes, Terraform/Terragrunt, Helm, ArgoCD, CI/CD pipelines, and Python automation - with a track record of GitOps-driven deployments, infrastructure-as-code, and cloud cost optimization. Security-first mindset shaped by penetration testing, SIEM engineering, and AI/LLM security reviews, bringing shift-left security into delivery pipelines. Runs a GitOps-driven homelab K3s cluster with self-hosted LLMs (Ollama, Gemma, Bonsai) for infrastructure and automation experimentation.

work.name: Indsysholdings
work.position: Senior Cybersecurity Engineer — DevSecOps & Cloud Security
work.summary: As a Senior Cybersecurity Engineer at Indsysholdings, I led infrastructure automation and platform operations across on-premise and cloud environments - owning Kubernetes, CI/CD, and cloud cost while embedding security into the delivery lifecycle.
highlights: Architected and operated a proof-of-concept multi-tenant ERPNext platform on a self-managed Kubernetes cluster, achieving logical compute and storage isolation per tenant using ArgoCD ApplicationSets and Helm - enabling scalable, GitOps-driven onboarding of new tenants without manual intervention.
Architected and implemented CI/CD pipelines for both monolithic and microservice applications, streamlining deployment workflows and reducing release cycle time.
Managed and optimized AWS cloud infrastructure for company services, achieving ~25-30% cost reduction through resource rightsizing, reserved instances, and usage audits.
Designed and implemented firewall rules and policies for local network infrastructure, enhancing perimeter security and access control.
Performed end-to-end Network VAPT for clients encompassing reconnaissance, vulnerability scanning, manual exploitation, and risk-rated remediation reporting.

work.name: IDfy
work.summary: As a Senior Information Security Engineer at IDfy, I drove DevSecOps automation across CI/CD and multi-cloud infrastructure - integrating security controls into pipelines, scaling SIEM coverage, and standardizing AI/LLM security reviews.
highlights: Implemented a CI compliance scanner running across all company repositories, surfacing policy drift early in the SDLC and shifting security left in the delivery pipeline.
Enhanced multi-cloud SIEM with automated asset onboarding, reducing manual provisioning effort and improving detection coverage.

Designed and operationalized an internal AI security intervention framework defining assessment triggers, threat models, and adversarial testing protocols for LLM-integrated features across internal and client-facing applications - standardizing the team's approach to AI/LLM security reviews aligned with OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications and MITRE ATLAS.

Conducted hands-on adversarial testing against LLM-integrated applications, validating risks across prompt injection, prompt leakage, training data poisoning, and model evasion; collaborated with AI/ML and Gen AI developers on mitigations.
Reduced false positives in Google Cloud Armor alerts by ~30% through detection tuning and signature refinement.
Implemented geo-fencing across company services/applications to reduce attack surface from out-of-scope regions.
Supported SOC 2 Type 2 audit by providing evidence for SIEM, vulnerability management, and incident response controls.

work.summary: As an Information Security Engineer at IDfy, I built and operated security automation and observability platforms across multi-cloud infrastructure, partnering with engineering teams to embed secure-by-default practices into the SDLC.
highlights: Established a unified security posture through a centralized multi-cloud SIEM [Wazuh] platform, reducing misconfigurations and increasing detection capabilities
Experimented with SOAR Deployment [Shuffle, Windmill.dev] for incident response automation
Developed and maintained custom security scripts and tools to automate routine tasks and improve efficiency.
Implemented Incident Response Playbook with focus on Web Application Firewall [WAF] and SIEM events
Collaborated with development teams to ensure secure coding practices and adherence to security standards.
Performed Multiple Vulnerability assessments for company assets, reducing the occurrence of high/critical severity by 75-80%

Conducted AI/LLM security assessments covering prompt injection, prompt leakage, training data poisoning, and model evasion - identifying risks in LLM-integrated applications and collaborating with development teams on mitigations
Responded to security incidents, conducted root cause analysis, and implemented corrective measures to prevent future occurrences.
Contributed to a secure and resilient organization by proactively identifying and addressing potential security risks.
Conducted Regular Information Security training sessions for company employees
Trained and empowered L1 SOC analysts to confidently handle critical cybersecurity incidents with speed and precision.
work.name: IDfy

work.summary: Gained practical cybersecurity experience as an intern at IDfy, where I contributed to enhancing application security. I performed comprehensive vulnerability assessments (VAPT) on critical applications, identifying and mitigating potential risks.
highlights: Collaborated with development teams to address identified vulnerabilities.
Provided detailed vulnerability reports with remediation recommendations.
Enhanced communication and collaboration between security and development teams.
work.name: IDfy

work.summary: Successfully created a Moodle Plugin that extracts various exam analytics.
highlights: Demonstrated expertise in creating a tailored Moodle [exam portal] plugin to address specific analytics requirements.
Successfully extracted relevant data from Moodle's database and performed in-depth analysis to derive meaningful insights.
Identified trends and patterns in student behavior and performance to support targeted interventions and improve overall learning outcomes.
work.name: NIIT University

projects.name: Personal Homelab - K3s Cluster
highlights: Self-hosted production-grade K3s cluster managing media, music, and LLM services with GitOps-driven continuous deployment via ArgoCD.
Maintained full infrastructure-as-code discipline - all service configurations version-controlled and reconciled automatically via ArgoCD.
Secured all external access through Cloudflare Tunnels, eliminating public port exposure and enforcing zero-trust network access.
Deployed and operated self-hosted LLM instances (Gemma 4B, Bonsai-4B on Ollama) as a personal automation backbone, integrated with homelab services for workflow automation.
Tech Stack: K3s, ArgoCD, Cloudflare Tunnels, Helm/Kustomize

projects.name: Automated Resume Pipeline
highlights: Designed a GitHub Actions CI pipeline that renders a JSON-sourced resume to PDF on every commit using a custom-modified ATS-optimized jsonresume theme.
Pipeline handles dependency caching, schema validation, HTML-to-PDF conversion, and automated versioned GitHub Release publication.
Forked and customized the jsonresume theme to meet specific ATS and visual requirements.
Tech Stack: GitHub Actions, Node.js, jsonresume, HTML/CSS

projects.name: Vaultify
highlights: Designed and developed a CLI-based secret manager to securely inject secrets into environment variables during local development without external dependencies like Docker or cloud KMS.
Participated in IDfy Hackathon
Tech Stack: Python, Redis, flask
Emphasized security-by-design principles: secrets are stored only in encrypted form; the master key is not stored on disk
Wrote comprehensive documentation and setup scripts to ensure developer onboarding within minutes
Delivered a clean and modular codebase, enabling future extensibility (e.g., GUI wrappers, plugin architecture).

projects.name: Swift Vote
highlights: Blockchain-based voting system specifically designed to address the legal requirements of a democratic process.
Tech Stack: Python, Solidity [ETH blockchain], SQLite
Created a voting environment where Vote tallies can be calculated directly from the blockchain, ensuring that results are tamper resistant
Created it with a zero trust policy approach.
Clear separation of concerns allows unit testing of core blockchain mechanics, mock voting flows, and integration testing of the full system.
Designed with a microservices architecture to enable scalable and maintainable deployments in the future
